LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VALLEY RELOCATION AND STORAGE OF NORTHERN CA INC'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE IS DENIED. A WIDER VENUE CHOICE IS PERMITTED IN ACTIONS AGAINST CORPORATIONS OR OTHER ENTITIES THAN IN ACTIONS AGAINST INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. ACTIONS AGAINST A CORPORATION ARE TRIABLE: (1) WHERE IT HAS ITS PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS (ITS "RESIDENCE" FOR VENUE PURPOSES); OR (2) WHERE THE CONTRACT WAS MADE OR TO BE PERFORMED (WHETHER SPECIFIED IN WRITING OR NOT); OR (3) WHERE THE OBLIGATION OR LIABILITY AROSE OR THE BREACH OCCURRED. [SEE CCP SEC. 395.5; ANAHEIM EXTRUSION CO. V. SUP.CT. (CLASSIC MOLDING CO.) (1985) 170 CA3D 1201, 120.] HERE, PLAINTIFF, A DELIVERY DRIVER FOR DEFENDANT VALLEY RELOCATION, PERFORMED HIS WORK IN THE COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THUS UNDER CCP SEC. 395.5 SAN FRANCISCO IS A PROPER VENUE. Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar May-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: JOSHUA G. KONECKY of Schneider Wallace Cottrell Konecky Wotkyns LLP, Tel No. (415) 421-7100 appearing for Defendant Valley Relocation and Storage of Northern CA Inc;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT VALLEY RELOCATION AND STORAGE OF NORTHERN CA INC'S Motion To Transfer Venue is denied. A wider venue choice is permitted in actions against corporations or other entities than in actions against individual defendants. Actions against a corporation are triable: (1) where it has its principal place of business (its "residence" for venue purposes); or (2) where the contract was made or to be performed (whether specified in writing or not); or (3) where the obligation or liability arose or the breach occurred. [See CCP Sec. 395.5; Anaheim Extrusion Co. v. Sup.Ct. (Classic Molding Co.) (1985) 170 CA3d 1201, 120.] Here, Plaintiff, a delivery driver for Defendant Valley Relocation, performed his work in the County of San Francisco, thus under CCP Sec. 395.5 San Francisco is a proper venue. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/LOT)
